feedback.claim: The Tiny Spec is too incomplete to support convergence checking; it contains only a single hashing statement and omits required normative details (scope/purpose, definitions, inputs/outputs, required behavior, and error/edge-case handling).
feedback.evidence: Draft contains only: "# Tiny Spec" and "## Hashing" with the single rule "The system hashes the draft."
feedback.recommended_change: Add normative sections that define: (1) what the "system" is and when hashing occurs, (2) what a "draft" is (exact bytes/encoding), (3) required behavior and acceptance criteria, and (4) error handling and edge cases. Ensure each requirement is testable and unambiguous.
feedback.severity_rationale: With only one vague sentence, independent implementations cannot converge on intended behavior, and reviewers cannot evaluate completeness or correctness.

feedback.claim: The hashing requirement is non-deterministic/under-specified because it does not define the hash algorithm, canonicalization, encoding, or output format.
feedback.evidence: The only hashing rule is: "The system hashes the draft."
feedback.recommended_change: Constrain the hashing spec by defining: the exact algorithm (e.g., SHA-256), the exact input byte sequence (including normalization/canonicalization rules and text encoding), and the output representation (hex/base64, lowercase/uppercase). State that hashing is deterministic over identical canonical inputs.
feedback.severity_rationale: Different implementations will choose different algorithms/encodings/canonicalizations, producing different hashes for the same draft, preventing convergence.
